metadata.title: Run the Three-Pass Verification Protocol with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Pass 3 is the one that stuck - the suite passed, but 'see all applications at a glance' took two clicks, and the fix was amending the SDD, not the build. I want to run all three passes on my own project. Help me: (1) list my Pass 2 edge cases, including the empty-state one that bit the tracker, (2) read my SDD user needs aloud and flag any the running build does not satisfy, and (3) for the failing need, decide whether to build it or amend the spec, and justify why a smaller satisfied spec beats a larger partial one.

props.command: Pass 3 is the one that stuck - the suite passed, but 'see all applications at a glance' took two clicks, and the fix was amending the SDD, not the build. I want to run all three passes on my own project. Help me: (1) list my Pass 2 edge cases, including the empty-state one that bit the tracker, (2) read my SDD user needs aloud and flag any the running build does not satisfy, and (3) for the failing need, decide whether to build it or amend the spec, and justify why a smaller satisfied spec beats a larger partial one.
props.segment: Run the Three-Pass Verification Protocol with

beats.narration_text: The test suite passes — and the user can't see their most important data at a glance. Pass 3 catches what Pass 1 and Pass 2 miss.
Tests verify code against tests. Only a human reading the SDD user needs aloud can catch the gap between what was built and what was needed. Pass 3 is the pass the test runner cannot run.
We run all three passes on the application tracker build. Pass 1 happy path. Pass 2 edge cases. Pass 3 SDD user needs read aloud.

props.command:
claude "Run three-pass verification on app tracker:
  Pass 1: happy path — does it run?
  Pass 2: edge cases — empty state, zero apps
  Pass 3: read SDD user needs aloud against running build
  Report: PASS/FAIL per pass with evidence"

beats.narration_text: Pass 1 passes. Pass 2 catches the empty-state edge case — zero applications shows an error instead of an empty list. Pass 3 flags: the SDD user need says 'see all applications at a glance' — the current UI requires two clicks to reach the list.

props.code:
# Verification Report — App Tracker

## Pass 1: Functional (happy path)
Result: PASS
Evidence: all 9 tests pass, app loads, CRUD works

## Pass 2: Edge Cases
Result: FAIL
Evidence: empty state (0 apps) shows TypeError
  instead of empty list message

## Pass 3: SDD User Needs (read aloud)
Result: FAIL
SDD need: 'see all applications at a glance'
Current UI: list is 2 clicks deep (Dashboard -> Apps -> List)
The need is not satisfied by the running build.

Recommendation: loop back to Pass 1 after fixes.

beats.narration_text: Pass 1 result: functional — the happy path runs. Pass 2 result: one edge case failure — empty state. Pass 3 result: one SDD gap — 'at a glance' user need not satisfied, list is two clicks deep. Loop-back to Pass 1 recommended.
We amend the SDD to remove the 'at a glance' user need and re-run Pass 3 — verify it now passes, showing that done is always relative to the spec.

props.command: claude "Amend SDD: remove 'at a glance' user need.\nRe-run Pass 3 against amended SDD.\nDoes Pass 3 now pass? Show which needs remain."

beats.narration_text: Pass 3 re-run: all three user needs now satisfied. The 'at a glance' need was removed from the SDD, not added to the build. The build is done — relative to the spec as amended. A smaller spec, satisfied, beats a larger spec, partially satisfied.

The three-pass protocol: functional, edge-case, SDD needs. The test runner handles Pass 1 and 2. Pass 3 requires a human to read the user needs aloud and ask whether the running build satisfies each one.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The three-pass protocol: functional, edge-case, SDD needs. The test runner handles Pass 1 and 2. Pass 3 requires a human to read the user needs aloud and ask whether the running build satisfies each one. Next: demonstrate the solve-verify asymmetry.

props.artifactHeading: Run the Three-Pass Verification Protocol with Claude Code
artifactLines: Pass 3 re-run: all three user needs now satisfied.
The three-pass protocol: functional, edge-case, SDD needs.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Run the Three-Pass Verification Protocol with Claude Code] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
